Archit Sharma

Posted on Jan 29

With over 100 million repositories on GitHub, it can be overwhelming to find the right repository for yourself.
But with a bit of research and some help from the GitHub community, you can easily find your way through the millions of repositories on GitHub.

𝐇𝐞𝐫𝐞 𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐚 𝐜𝐮𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐥𝐢𝐬𝐭 𝐨𝐟 𝐆𝐢𝐭𝐇𝐮𝐛 𝐫𝐞𝐩𝐨𝐬𝐢𝐭𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐞𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐲 𝐡𝐞𝐥𝐩 𝐲𝐨𝐮 𝐭𝐨 𝐚𝐝𝐯𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐜𝐚𝐫𝐞𝐞𝐫 as a Developer:

1. Project Based Learning

A list of programming tutorials in which aspiring software developers learn how to build an application from scratch. These tutorials are divided into different primary programming languages. Tutorials may involve multiple technologies and languages.

2. Devops Exercises

If you want to work as a DevOps engineer, learning some of the concepts mentioned in this repository will be beneficial.
This repository Contains questions and exercises on a variety of technical DevOps and SRE topics.

3. SRE Interview Preparation Guide

This repository is an attempt to consolidate useful resources for Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) interview preparation.

4. The System Design Primer

Learning how to design scalable systems will help you become a better engineer. System design is a broad topic. There is a vast amount of resources scattered throughout the web on system design principles.

This repository is an organized collection of resources to help you learn how to build systems at scale.

5. Sysadmin Interview Questions

A collection of Linux System Administrator/DevOps interview questions. Feel free to contribute via pull requests, issues, or email messages.

6. Every Programmer Should Know

A collection of (mostly) technical things every software developer should know about.

7. Coding Interview University

A complete computer science study plan to become a software engineer.

8. Developer Roadmap

Developer Roadmap is a community effort to create roadmaps, guides and other educational content to help guide the developers in picking up the path and guide their learnings.
Interactive roadmaps, guides and other educational content to help developers grow in their careers.

9. Test Your Sysadmin Skills

A collection of Linux Sysadmin Test Questions and Answers. Test your knowledge and skills in different fields with these Q/A.

10. Frontend Dev Bookmarks

Manually curated collection of resources for frontend web developers.

If you found this useful, consider:

✅ Follow @iarchitsharma for more content like this
✅ Follow me on Twitter where I share free resources on regular basis!

item.title: 理解 javascript:void(0) 语句 | Understanding the javascript:void(0) Statement
item.description:
有时，您可能会遇到包含使用href="JavaScript:void(0);"的 HTML 文档。 在<a>元素中。 您可以有效地消除这些负面结果，因为它返回未定义的原始值。

当您需要在链接中调用 JavaScript 时，单击链接通常会导致浏览器加载新页面或刷新当前页面或丢失当前滚动位置。 但是，如果您已将一些 JavaScript 附加到链接，您可能不希望出现此行为。

一种将 JavaScript 代码直接嵌入 HTML 文档的方法。 它可以用作超链接的 href 属性的值或事件处理程序（如 onclick）的值。

例如，如果您有一个链接需要在单击时执行特定的 JavaScript 功能，而不是导航到不同的页面，您可以使用“javascript:”伪 URL：

 <a href="javascript:myFunction()">Click here</a>

当用户点击链接时，JavaScript 函数myFunction()将被执行，页面不会离开。

 <a href="javascript:console.log('Code of Relevancy');">Click me</a>

请务必注意，此方法可能存在安全风险，因为它允许在用户计算机上执行潜在的恶意代码，因此应谨慎使用。

无效（0）

它经常用于通过使用void(0)或void 0来简单地获取未定义的原始值。 但是，值得注意的是，在大多数情况下，全局变量 undefined 可以用作 void 运算符的替代，前提是它没有被重新分配给非默认值。

例子

<a href="JavaScript:void(0)">Click me, nothing will happen</a>

此锚标记指定带有javascript:void(0) URL 的超链接。 点击时执行JavaScript函数void，返回undefined ，对页面没有影响。 使用javascript:void(0)作为 href 值的目的是防止页面在点击链接时刷新和更改 URL。 它通常在需要链接但不需要执行任何操作时使用。

 <a href="#section2">Go to Section 2</a> <a href="#">Click me</a>

此锚标记指定一个 URL 为#的超链接。 它还用于在同一页面内创建内部链接，允许用户跳转到页面内的特定部分。

请考虑通过订阅我们的频道来支持我们。 非常感谢您的支持，这将帮助我们继续创作供您欣赏的内容。 预先感谢您对我们的支持！

推特



原文:

Occasionally, you may come across an HTML document that includes the use of href="JavaScript:void(0);" within an <a> element. This usage of JavaScript void is often employed when inserting expressions into a web page may result in undesired side-effects.

You can effectively eliminate these negative outcomes as it returns the undefined primitive value.

A frequent use case for JavaScript:void(0) is with hyperlinks. When you need to call JavaScript within a link, clicking the link typically causes the browser to either load a new page or refresh the current page or lost the current scroll position. However, if you have attached some JavaScript to the link, you may not want this behavior to occur.

javascript:

javascript: is a pseudo URL. A way to embed JavaScript code directly into an HTML document. It can be used as the value of a hyperlink's href attribute or as the value of an event handler such as onclick.

For example, if you have a link that needs to perform a specific JavaScript function when clicked, instead of navigating to a different page, you can use the "javascript:" pseudo URL:

<a href="javascript:myFunction()">Click here</a>

When the user clicks on the link, the JavaScript function myFunction() will be executed, and the page will not navigate away.

<a href="javascript:console.log('Code of Relevancy');">Click me</a>

It is important to note that this method can be a security risk as it allows for potentially malicious code to be executed on the user's computer, so it should be used with caution.

void(0)

The void operator is a valuable tool in JavaScript that evaluates an expression and returns the undefined value. It is frequently used to simply obtain the undefined primitive value by using void(0) or void 0. However, it's worth noting that the global variable undefined can be used as a substitute for the void operator in most cases, provided it has not been reassigned to a non-default value.

Examples 

<a href="JavaScript:void(0)">Click me, nothing will happen</a>

This anchor tag specifies a hyperlink with a javascript:void(0) URL. When clicked, the JavaScript function void is executed, which returns undefined and has no effect on the page. The purpose of using javascript:void(0) as the href value is to prevent the page from refreshing and changing the URL when the link is clicked. It's often used when a link is desired but no action is needed to happen.

<a href="#section2">Go to Section 2</a>

<a href="#">Click me</a>

This anchor tag specifies a hyperlink with a URL of #. The # symbol is often used as a placeholder URL, and clicking on the link jumps to the top of the page. It's also used to create an internal link within the same page, allowing the user to jump to a specific section within the page.

🍀Conclusion:
javascript:void(0) is used as a placeholder URL when a link is needed but no action is desired, while # is used as a placeholder URL to jump to the top of the page or to create internal links within the same page.

item.title: 用于回归和分类的 K 最近邻算法 | The K-Nearest Neighbors Algorithm for regression and classification
item.description:
该算法的工作原理是找到与给定测试数据点最接近的 k 个数据点（邻居），并根据它们的标签/值进行预测。 预测通常是 k 个最近邻之间的平均值（用于回归）或多数类标签（用于分类）。

用数学术语来说，KNN 是一种非参数方法。 给定一个 d 维特征空间中包含 N 个标记点的训练数据集，其中每个点由其 d 个特征值和一个类标签表示，KNN 算法的工作原理如下：

  1. 对于具有特征值 x 的新测试数据点，使用欧几里德或其他距离度量来计算 x 与 N 个训练数据点中的每一个之间的距离。

  2. 根据距离选择 K 个最近的邻居，其中 K 是用户定义的参数。

  3. 对于分类问题，将 K 个最近的邻居分配给它们各自的类标签，并将多数类标签用作 x 的预测。 这可以表示为：

  1. 对于回归问题，K 个最近的邻居用于通过取其标签的平均值来预测 x 的值。 这可以表示为：

总之，KNN 是一种简单而强大的算法，易于理解和实现。 它不对底层数据分布做任何假设，适用于广泛的应用。 然而，KNN 的性能会受到 K 的选择和使用的距离度量的影响。 对数据进行预处理并对特征进行归一化也很重要，以防止某个特征对结果的影响。 此外，不建议将 KNN 用于大型数据集，因为计算时间会随着数据集的大小线性增加。 总体而言，KNN 是许多分类和回归问题的良好起点，但重要的是评估其性能并在需要时考虑其他算法。

主帖： 完整数据科学训练营

原文:

K-nearest neighbors (KNN) is a supervised learning algorithm used for classification and regression. The algorithm works by finding the k closest data points (neighbors) to a given test data point and making a prediction based on their labels/values. The prediction is typically the average (for regression) or the majority class label (for classification) among the k nearest neighbors.

In mathematical terms, KNN is a non-parametric method. Given a training dataset of N labeled points in a d-dimensional feature space, where each point is represented by its d feature values and a class label, the KNN algorithm works as follows:

  1. For a new test data point with feature values x, the Euclidean or other distance metric is used to calculate the distance between x and each of the N training data points.

  2. The K nearest neighbors are selected based on the distances, where K is a user-defined parameter.

  3. For a classification problem, the K nearest neighbors are assigned to their respective class labels and the majority class label is used as the prediction for x. This can be represented as:

  1. For a regression problem, the K nearest neighbors are used to predict the value of x by taking the average of their labels. This can be represented as:

In conclusion, KNN is a simple yet powerful algorithm that is easy to understand and implement. It does not make any assumptions about the underlying data distribution and is suitable for a wide range of applications. However, the performance of KNN can be affected by the choice of K and the distance metric used. It is also important to preprocess the data and normalize the features to prevent the influence of one feature on the results. In addition, KNN is not recommended for large datasets as the computation time increases linearly with the size of the dataset. Overall, KNN is a good starting point for many classification and regression problems, but it is important to evaluate its performance and consider other algorithms if needed.

item.title: 旋转木马项目与反应 | Carousel project with react
item.description:
戈比纳特·瓦拉塔拉詹

发表于 1 月 31 日

该项目有助于理解 useState、map 和三元运算符的概念，如何有效地使用它们

我有一个像这样的图像列表

const sampleImages = [ "https://images.pexels.com/photos/3836292/pexels-photo-3836292.jpeg?auto=compress&cs=tinysrgb&dpr=2&w=500", "https://images.pexels.com/photos/2792157/pexels-photo-2792157.jpeg?auto=compress&cs=tinysrgb&dpr=2&w=500", "https://images.pexels.com/photos/1166209/pexels-photo-1166209.jpeg?auto=compress&cs=tinysrgb&dpr=2&w=500", "https://images.pexels.com/photos/1661179/pexels-photo-1661179.jpeg?auto=compress&cs=tinysrgb&dpr=2&w=500", "https://images.pexels.com/photos/45201/kitty-cat-kitten-pet-45201.jpeg?auto=compress&cs=tinysrgb&dpr=2&w=500" ];

使用的操作：

  1. 使用状态
  2. 地图
  3. 三元运算符

如果 gobinath 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

报告其他不当行为

不屈不挠的 gobinath 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。

item.title: 构建按需送餐应用程序需要多少成本 | How Much Does It Cost To Build an On-Demand Food Delivery App
item.description:
十年前，将餐厅的食物送到人们家门口的概念似乎有些牵强。 然而，由于移动应用程序的强大功能，我们现在只需点击几下就可以在线订购我们最喜欢的商品。

但是，此博客不会告诉您应该尝试使用哪种送餐应用程序。 相反，此博​​客讨论按需送餐应用程序的开发成本、相关参数、时间表和应用程序的功能。

我们将回顾要点并详细讨论每个因素。

有几个因素决定了送餐应用程序的开发成本。 例如，

  • 要使用的平台
  • 应用程序中的功能
  • 人力资源
  • 测试
  • 维护更新

大多数在线应用程序成本估算器都遵循以下一般规则：

应用开发成本 = 总时间 * 小时费率

按需外卖应用程序开发的总时间取决于以下主要因素：

  • 应用程序的功能
  • 要使用的平台
  • 人力资源
  • 技术栈
  • 测试和发布

一、特点

一个简单的按需送餐应用程序至少应具有以下功能。

客户端：

  • 登录
  • 搜索菜单
  • 大车
  • 支付整合
  • 订单跟踪
  • 评级和评论
  • 客户支持

餐厅方面：

  • 登录
  • 菜单
  • 推送通知
  • 检查评级和评论

交货方

  • 登记
  • 订单管理
  • 更新订单状态

管理端：

  • 管理员登录
  • 餐厅管理
  • 申请管理
  • 支付管理
  • 折扣和优惠
  • 技术援助

永远记住，开发时间与应用程序功能和复杂性成正比。 添加的功能越多，开发周期就越长。

您可以在此处选择开发本机或跨平台应用程序。

本机应用程序是特定于平台的。 因此，开发时间会更长。 原生 iOS 和 Android 开发总共需要 560 到 650 小时。

  • 跨平台应用开发

跨平台应用程序与平台无关。 相同的代码库可跨 Android 和 iOS 执行。 这缩短了项目时间表并节省了大量开发时间。 所提供的技能集在决定应用程序的成本和质量方面起着重要作用。 在您的应用程序上工作的人员是决定其价格的最重要因素之一。

技术娴熟且经验丰富的开发人员在项目时间表上产生重大影响。 他们的成本可能比不熟练或没有经验的开发人员多一点，但他们具有更高的质量和保证。

下一步是在本地和离岸移动应用程序开发公司之间做出决定，以开发按需送餐应用程序。 这两种组件的价格范围很广。 虽然雇用内部开发人员是负担得起的，但雇用离岸开发人员的成本很高。

将技术堆栈视为开发人员用来设计、构建和实施按需送餐应用程序的编程工具、框架、指南和方法。

大多数企业将技术堆栈选择留给他们的开发人员或他们将项目外包给的移动应用程序开发公司。

本机应用程序的技术堆栈不同于跨平台应用程序的技术堆栈。 由于技术堆栈的复杂性和规模各不相同，开发时间和成本也各不相同。

测试成本占开发成本的 10% 到 20%。 根据不同的估计，确切的数字可能在 5,000 美元到 25,000 美元或更多之间。

您越早发现并解决错误，您花在应用测试上的钱就越少。 换句话说，发布后测试的成本非常高，因为它需要重新设计整个模块。

一旦您的应用程序准备好上线，您还必须支付出版费。 要在 Apple 保持活跃的开发者帐户，您必须支付 100 美元的年费。 您必须支付 25 美元的一次性费用才能在 Google Play for Android 上发布应用程序。 当你第一次设置你的账户时，谷歌和苹果一样，收取应用销售收入的 30%。

开发人员的费用会显着影响外卖应用程序的开发成本。 美国和欧洲的开发者要求的费用远高于亚洲的开发者。

为了让您快速了解，以下是不同地区软件开发人员的小时收费：

  • 欧洲每小时 25-50 美元
  • 拉丁美洲每小时 30-50 美元
  • 亚洲每小时 18-40 美元
  • 在印度每小时 10-30 美元

由于客户的便利，按需移动服务蓬勃发展。 只需轻扫一下手机，即可获得出租车、电影票、食品、酒店预订和其他服务。 这就是送餐应用程序如今如此受欢迎的原因。

对于按需送餐应用程序开发，收入来源可能很多。

餐厅和在线送餐应用程序可以根据订单大小按照预先确定的佣金率合作。

在外卖应用程序中投放广告可能需要将餐厅置于搜索结果的顶部一段时间。

选择合适的商业模式对于创建送餐应用至关重要，因为它直接影响产品/市场契合度，并决定解决方案能否在竞争中生存下来。

我们将讨论三种主要的商业模式，

在此模型中，企业主与第三方交付服务合作。 送货公司也可以设定价格。 食品店在平台上注册，只负责准备食物，而送货由送货伙伴负责。 大多数流行的应用程序，如 Swiggy、UberEats、Zomato 和 DoorDash，都在这种模型上运行。

在此模型中，交付可能由应用程序管理员和餐厅共同管理。 许多餐馆已经有送货员，并且正在寻找一种方法将他们的菜单放到网上。

此应用程序允许餐馆使用该应用程序的送货服务或送餐。

这种商业模式允许更广泛的餐厅参与，因为它为他们提供了自助送货选项，从而为他们节省了第三方送货服务的费用。 此外，更全面的食品选择范围可改善客户体验。

送餐应用程序所有者负责库存模型下的一切。 一切都由送餐公司处理，从启动送餐网站/应用程序到准备订单和向客户送餐。 平台所有者负责整个价值链。 必胜客、达美乐和麦当劳使用库存商业模式。

复杂的定制（多功能）送餐应用程序的开发成本从 50,000 美元起。 开发食品应用程序的平均成本是：

  • 原生应用程序 52,250 美元至 59,800 美元
  • 跨平台应用程序为 46,300 美元至 52,000 美元。

请记住，我们没有考虑应用程序维护、升级或托管成本等因素。 然而，这些变量因服务提供商而异。 聘请送餐应用程序开发公司时，请不要忘记确认这些费用。

最好从 5-10 家不同的公司获取报价，然后选择最符合您的预算和期望的一家。 此外，不要忘记确保应用程序开发公司在食品技术行业拥有良好的业绩记录。 一切顺利！



原文:

A decade ago, the concept of restaurant food being delivered to people's doorstep seemed far-fetched. However, thanks to the power of mobile apps, we can now order our favorite online with just a few clicks.

However, this blog doesn't tell you which food delivery app you should try. Instead, this blog discusses on-demand food delivery app development costs, dependent parameters, timeline and features of the app.

We will go over the essentials and discuss every factor in detail.

Several factors determine food delivery app development costs. For example,

  • Platform to be used
  • Features in the application
  • Human Resource
  • Testing
  • Maintenance and update

Most of the online app cost estimators follow this general rule:

App Development Cost = Total Time * Hourly Rate

The total time for on-demand food delivery app development depends on the following major factors:

  • App’s features
  • Platform to be used
  • Human resource
  • Technology stack
  • Testing and publication

1. Features

A simple on-demand food delivery app should at least have the following features.

Customer Side:

  • Login
  • Search Menu
  • Cart
  • Payment Integration
  • Order Tracking
  • Rating and Reviews
  • Customer Support

Restaurant Side:

  • Login
  • Menu
  • Push Notification
  • Checking Rating and Reviews

Delivery Side

  • Registration
  • Order Management
  • Updating Order Status

Admin Side:

  • Admin Login
  • Restaurant Management
  • Application Management
  • Payment Management
  • Discount and Offers
  • Technical Assistance

Always remember that development time is directly proportional to application features and complexity. The more features you add, the longer the development cycle.

2. Platforms

You have the option of developing native or cross-platform app here.

Native apps are platform-specific. Hence, the development time will be more. Both native iOS and Android development take between 560 and 650 hours overall.

  • Cross-platform app development

Cross-platform apps are platform-independent. The same code base is executable across Android and iOS. This shortens the project timeline and saves a lot of development hours. The entire on-demand food delivery app development takes between 470 and 550 development hours for iOS and Android.

3. Human Resource

The skill set on offer plays a significant role in determining not just the cost but the quality of the app. The individuals that work on your app are one of the most important aspects that decide its price.

Skilled and experienced developers make a significant difference in the project timeline. They may cost a little more than unskilled or inexperienced developers, but they come with higher quality and assurance.

The next step is to decide between a local and offshore mobile app development company for on-demand food delivery app development. Both of these components have a wide range of prices. While hiring in-house developers is affordable, hiring offshore developers is costly.

4. Technology Stack

Think of the technology stack as the programming tools, frameworks, guides, and methodology developers use to design, build and implement an on-demand food delivery app.

Most businesses leave the technology stack selection to their developers or the mobile app development companies to which they outsource the project.

The tech stack for a native app differs from that of a cross-platform app. As the tech stack varies in complexity and scale, so does the development time and cost.

5. Testing and Publication

The cost for testing ranges from 10% to 20% of the development cost. According to different estimates, the exact figures could range from $5,000 to $25,000 or more.

The sooner you detect and resolve a bug, the less money you'll spend on app testing. In other words, post-launch testing is extremely costly because it necessitates the redesign of entire modules.

You must also pay publication fees once your app is ready to go live. To keep an active developer account with Apple, you must pay a $100 annual fee. You must pay a one-time cost of $25 to publish apps on Google Play for Android. When you first set up your account, Google, like Apple, takes thirty percent of app sale revenue.

The developer's fees significantly impact food delivery app development cost. Developers in the United States and Europe demand far higher fees than those in Asia.

To provide you with a quick overview, here are the hourly charges of software developers in different regions:

  • $25-50 per hour in Europe
  • $30-50 per hour in Latin America
  • $18-40 per hour in Asia
  • $10-30 per hour in India

On-demand mobile services thrive because of customer convenience. Taxis, movie tickets, food, hotel reservations, and other services may be obtained with a single swipe of your phone. This is why food delivery apps are so popular these days.

For on-demand food delivery app development, the revenue sources can be many.

- Delivery fees: Depending on the distance, delivery fees can be a percentage of the order price or a flat amount.

- Surcharges During Peak Hours: UberEats charges a surcharge during lunch and supper. UberEats restricts menus and locations during busy hours and charges an additional cost to make them available.

- Commission %: A commission percentage is another way to monetize a food delivery app. Restaurants and online food delivery apps can work together on predetermined commission rates based on order size.

- In-app Advertising: For a fee, a delivery app can advertise eateries within the app. Advertising in a delivery app can entail putting a restaurant at the top of the search results for a set amount of time.

Selecting an appropriate business model is essential in creating a food delivery app since it directly impacts product/market fit and determines if the solution will survive the competition.

We'll go over the three primary business models,

- Order and Delivery Model

In this model, business owners partner with a third-party delivery service. The delivery company can also set its prices. Food outlets register on the platform and are only responsible for food preparation while the delivery is taken care of by the delivery partners. Most popular apps like Swiggy, UberEats, Zomato, and DoorDash operate on this model.

- Integrated Model

Delivery may be managed by both the app admin and the restaurants in this model. Many restaurants already have their delivery staff and are looking for a way to bring their menus online.

This app allows eateries to use the app's delivery services or deliver their food.

This business model allows a broader range of restaurants to participate because it provides them with a self-delivery option, which saves them money on third-party delivery services. Furthermore, a more comprehensive range of food options improves the client experience.

- Inventory Business Model

The food delivery app owners are in-charge of everything under the inventory model. Everything is handled by the food delivery company, from launching the food delivery website/app to preparing orders and delivering meals to customers. The platform owner is in charge of the entire value chain. Pizza Hut, Domino's, and McDonald's use the inventory business model.

The development cost of a complex, custom (multi-feature) food delivery app starts from $50,000. The development of an MVP could take 4-6 months (a food delivery app based on the Inventory Business Model). The average cost of developing a food app is:

  • $52,250 to $59,800 for Native apps
  • $46,300 to $52,000 for cross-platform apps.

Please remember that we haven't factored in things like app maintenance, upgrades, or hosting costs. These variables, however, differ from one service provider to the next. Don't forget to confirm these charges when hiring a food delivery app development company.

It's a good idea to acquire quotations from 5-10 different companies and then pick the one that best fits your budget and expectations. Also, do not forget to ensure that the app development company has a proven track record in the food tech business. All the best!
item.author: hims_verma

item.title: 如何在 Flutter 中创建自定义卡片？ | How To Create Custom Cards In Flutter?
item.description:
您想探索如何在 Flutter 中创建自定义卡片吗？ 如果是，请通读本指南并收集所有必要的详细信息。 好吧，Flutter 应用程序为 Flutter 卡片小部件使用自定义卡片的形式。 自定义卡片是动态创建自定义卡片有效开发的有效因素。 他们使用自定义卡片等工具有效地开发应用程序和自定义卡片的 Flutter 卡片小部件。

在 Flutter 开发中创建自定义卡片使用与 Flutter 卡片小部件类似的编码开发。 应用程序开发中的动态 Flutter 卡片小部件使用各种 Flutter 包，因此安装所有包以有效地动态创建自定义卡片。

在 Flutter 中创建自定义卡片

自定义卡片是用于 Flutter 程序的语法，可以有效地嵌入自定义卡片应用程序的服务器代码。 通常，基于服务器的代码对于在 Flutter 卡片小部件构建中创建动态 Web 内容非常有用，而网页则写入自定义卡片的浏览器编码。

创建与客户端类似的自定义卡片，专为用户定义的关键字而设计，以更有效地定义 Flutter 卡片小部件。 在这里，当网页被调用时，服务器成功地执行页面中的基于服务器的代码，甚至在页面在网络浏览器中执行以显示结果之前。

通过使用定制卡，使用代码运行服务器非常容易，它们提供了访问数据库元素以执行基于 Web 的应用程序的平台。

它们在这里呈现。

使用Flutter卡片小部件的网页开发，必须使用Flutter应用程序的开发。

为了有效开发 Flutter 卡片小部件，自定义卡片的使用与 Flutter 应用程序和应用程序开发的标准用法更有效地结合使用。

应用程序中使用的概念或使用来自广泛应用程序开发的标准动态 Flutter 卡小部件的应用程序开发的相同相似概念。

为了在 Flutter 中使用常见的编程任务创建自定义卡片，自定义卡片正在这些平台上使用。 在 Flutter 上开发应用程序时，您想了解的一些最有效的需求是自定义卡片、Flutter 创建自定义卡片，以及用于了解如何使用自定义卡片创建自定义卡片。

在自定义卡片中创建页面

自定义卡片用于使用 Flutter 应用程序开发动态 Web 应用程序。 自定义卡片是动态web应用的有效封装，因此在Flutter项目中的Flutter卡片小部件中必不可少。 要制作这些语法，请使用以下步骤。

对于动态 Flutter 卡片小部件，创建自定义卡片。 如果它已经打开，请单击站点并选择更有效地重新打开访问文件的过程。

之后，选择文件的用途以供应用程序在工作区中使用。 在该弹出框中，选择有效收益的新选项。 打开页面后，使用各种进程。 开发用于在自定义卡中有效创建自定义卡的编码。

使用有效的 Flutter 自定义卡片

自定义卡片在 Flutter 的编程平台上有效运行。 自定义卡片使用 Flutter Web 应用程序中使用的各种自定义卡片。 一些有用的自定义卡片可以通过自定义卡片和自定义卡片中的验证过程访问。 那么，Flutter 应用程序开发人员需要更广泛的代码开发知识。

因此，仅使用自定义卡片可以更有效地在 Flutter 卡片小部件的 Web 应用程序中更轻松地进行编码。 有这么多简单的工具，Flutter card widgets提供了自定义卡片使用的应用接口，在Flutter中安装好自定义卡片后，使用它们进行有效的开发，使用镜像以更有效的方式进行全球访问的发展。

使用动态 Flutter 卡片小部件的最有效需求是使用自定义卡片或进行一些复杂的编码实现。 要创建自定义卡片，必须了解使用 Flutter 应用程序迁移的 Flutter 卡片小部件应用程序中自定义卡片有效性的语法。

程序可以像 Flutter 一样使用任何字符或句子，但 Flutter 框架应该使用任何字母数字字符启动，并且不包括任何保留字符或空白字符的使用。

使用 Flutter 卡片小部件

其他一些类型的数据用于 Flutter 如何创建自定义卡片程序，此处介绍了其中一些。

例子

import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; const Color darkBlue = Color.fromARGB(255, 18, 32, 47); void main() { runApp(MyApp()); } class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp( debugShowCheckedModeBanner: false, home: Scaffold( appBar: AppBar( title: const Text('Custom card'), centerTitle: true, ), //AppBar body: Center( child: Card( shadowColor: Colors.black, color: Colors.greenAccent[100], child: SizedBox( width: 300, height: 150, child: Padding( padding: const EdgeInsets.all(20.0), child: Column( children: [ const Text( 'Custom card', style: TextStyle( fontSize: 30, fontWeight: FontWeight.w500, ), //Textstyle ), //Text const SizedBox( height: 10, ), //SizedBox const Text( 'This is the card widget', style: TextStyle( fontSize: 15, ), //Textstyle ), //Text const SizedBox( height: 10, ), //SizedBox SizedBox( width: 100, child: ElevatedButton( onPressed: () => 'Null', child: Padding( padding: const EdgeInsets.all(4), child: Row( children: const [ Icon(Icons.touch_app), Text('Visit') ], ), ), ), ) ], ), ), ), ), ), ), ); } }

输出

包起来

本文通过简要说明让您了解它对自定义卡片的影响、Flutter 卡片小部件的使用以及如何在 Flutter 卡片中创建自定义页面。 但是，可以使用 Flutter 卡片小部件完成各种操作。 然而，当与其他 Flutter Widgets 合并时，它构建了一个漂亮的设计和完整的可重用小部件，可以在应用程序开发的任何地方使用。 因此，小部件是构建块和完美组合，可以在您的应用程序中发挥最佳作用。

常见问题 (FAQ)

该卡片是 Flutter 中的内置小部件，其设计源自 Google 的 Material Design 库。 这个小部件在屏幕上的功能是它是无害的空间或带圆角的面板，并且在下侧具有最小的高度。

您必须通过获取 boxShadow 属性将卡片包装在容器中以将阴影应用于卡片小部件。

卡片小部件提供了一个灵活且可扩展的内容容器，您可以在其中查看其相关信息的各个部分。 然而，它的内容可以包含多个小部件，如文本、图像、地图和按钮。 因此，从不同的预定义卡片模板中选择空白模板来设计您自己的模板。

您可以用 <a> 包裹块元素。 它是有效的 HTML5，将使您的卡片可点击。 因此，您需要调整块内文本的 CSS 规则。

Example 

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
const Color darkBlue = Color.fromARGB(255, 18, 32, 47);
void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}
class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      debugShowCheckedModeBanner: false,
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(
          title: const Text('Custom card'),
          centerTitle: true,
        ), //AppBar
        body: Center(
          child: Card(
            shadowColor: Colors.black,
            color: Colors.greenAccent[100],
            child: SizedBox(
              width: 300,
              height: 150,
              child: Padding(
                padding: const EdgeInsets.all(20.0),
                child: Column(
                  children: [
                    const Text(
                      'Custom card',
                      style: TextStyle(
                        fontSize: 30,
                        fontWeight: FontWeight.w500,
                      ), //Textstyle
                    ), //Text
                    const SizedBox(
                      height: 10,
                    ), //SizedBox
                    const Text(
                      'This is the card widget',
                      style: TextStyle(
                        fontSize: 15,
                      ), //Textstyle
                    ), //Text
                    const SizedBox(
                      height: 10,
                    ), //SizedBox
                    SizedBox(
                      width: 100,
                      child: ElevatedButton(
                        onPressed: () => 'Null',
                        child: Padding(
                          padding: const EdgeInsets.all(4),
                          child: Row(
                            children: const [
                              Icon(Icons.touch_app),
                              Text('Visit')
                            ],
                          ),
                        ),
                      ),
                    )
                  ],
                ),
              ),
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

Output

Wrapping Up

This article gives you an idea of its impact on custom cards, the use of Flutter card widgets, and how to create custom pages in Flutter cards with a brief description. However, when merged with the other Flutter Widgets, it builds a beautiful design and the complete reusable widget ready to use anywhere in the app development. Hence, the widgets are the building blocks and the perfect combination which brings out the best in your app.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

1. Why do we use the cards in Flutter development?

The card is the built-in widget in Flutter that derives its design from Google’s Material Design Library. The functionality of this widget on screen is that it is the inoffensive space or panel with rounded corners and has minimal elevation on the lower side.

2. How to set the card shadow in the Flutter project?

You have to wrap a card in the container to apply the shadow to the card widget by getting the boxShadow property.

3. What are the widget cards?

The card widget gives a flexible and extensible content container in which you can view the various bits of its related information. However, its content can consist of multiple widgets like text, images, maps and buttons. Hence, choose from distinct predefined card templates to select the blank template to design your own.

4. How can you create a clickable card?

You can wrap the block elements with < a >. It is valid HTML5 and will render your card clickable. Hence, you are required to adjust the CSS rules for the text which is inside the block.

item.title: 减少和管理固体废物的十大方法 | Top 10 Ways To Reduce And Manage Solid Waste
item.description:
减少**：管理固体废物最有效的方法之一是首先减少产生的数量。 这可以通过减少消费、使用可重复使用的物品而不是一次性物品以及购买包装最少的产品来实现。

  • 重复使用：重复使用物品而不是丢弃它们可以大大减少产生的固体废物量。 例如，使用可重复使用的袋子、容器和瓶子可以减少对一次性塑料制品的需求。
  • 回收：回收是管理固体废物最有效的方法之一。 纸张、塑料、玻璃和金属等许多材料都可以回收利用并用于制造新产品。
  • 堆肥：食物残渣和庭院垃圾等有机废物可以堆肥，为园艺创造营养丰富的土壤。
  • 焚烧：焚烧是燃烧固体废物以减少其体积并将其转化为灰烬、热量和气体的过程。
  • 填埋：填埋是最常见的废物处理方法，专门设计用于容纳和隔离废物，防止污染。
  • 垃圾焚烧发电厂（WTE） ：垃圾焚烧发电厂利用固体废物燃烧产生的热量来发电。
  • 资源回收：资源回收是从金属、塑料和玻璃等固体废弃物中提取有价值资源的过程。
  • 危险废物管理：危险废物必须与其他类型的固体废物分开管理，因为它可能危害人类健康和环境。
  • 教育和意识：教育和意识在固体废物管理中起着至关重要的作用。 通过教育人们减少、再利用和回收的重要性，以及废物管理不当对环境的负面影响，我们可以鼓励更负责任的废物管理实践。

    • 一旦暂停，ananddhawan 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    解除暂停后，ananddhawan 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，ananddhawan 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 ananddhawan 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    降低 ananddhawan 发布的所有帖子的可见度

    原文:

    **

    1. Reduce**: One of the most effective ways to manage solid waste is to reduce the amount that is produced in the first place. This can be done by consuming less, using reusable items instead of disposable ones, and buying products with minimal packaging.
    2. Reuse: Reusing items instead of discarding them can greatly reduce the amount of solid waste that is produced. For example, using reusable bags, containers, and bottles can reduce the need for disposable plastic items.
    3. Recycle: Recycling is one of the most effective ways to manage solid waste. Many materials such as paper, plastic, glass, and metal can be recycled and used to make new products.
    4. Composting: Organic waste such as food scraps and yard waste can be composted to create nutrient-rich soil for gardening.
    5. Incineration: Incineration is the process of burning solid waste to reduce its volume and convert it into ash, heat, and gas.
    6. Landfilling: Landfilling is the most common method of waste disposal, specially designed to contain and isolate waste, preventing pollution.
    7. Waste-to-Energy (WTE) plants: Waste-to-energy plants use the heat generated from burning solid waste to produce electricity.
    8. Resource Recovery: Resource recovery is the process of extracting valuable resources from solid waste such as metals, plastics, and glass.
    9. Hazardous Waste Management: Hazardous waste must be managed separately from other types of solid waste due to its potential to harm human health and the environment.
    10. Education and Awareness: Education and awareness play a crucial role in solid waste management. By educating people about the importance of reducing, reusing, and recycling, and the negative impact of mismanaging waste on the environment, we can encourage more responsible waste management practices.

    Sumudu Siriwardana - Jan 30

    Aravind kumar TS - Jan 29

    Techsolutionstuff - Jan 30

item.title: 每个软件开发人员都必须熟悉的 10 个基本平台和工具！ | 10 Essential Platforms and Tools Every Software Developer Must Be Familiar With !
item.description:
随着软件开发领域的不断发展和扩展，开发人员要跟上最新的工具和技术可能会面临挑战。 然而，熟悉最基本的平台和工具对于在该领域取得成功至关重要。 无论您是经验丰富的开发人员还是刚刚起步，了解软件开发的最新进展都非常重要。 在这篇文章中，我们将探讨每位软件开发人员必须熟悉的 10 大基本平台和工具，才能在游戏中保持领先地位。

无论您是要托管新应用程序、在云中存储数据，还是管理复杂的基础设施，AWS、GCP 和 Azure 都能满足您的需求。

无论您是单独工作还是作为团队的一员，Git 都可以帮助您跟踪代码库、与他人协作并在必要时恢复更改。

使用 GitHub，您可以将代码存储在云中、与他人协作并跟踪代码库的更改。

使用 Docker，您可以创建与底层基础架构隔离的容器，从而更轻松地管理和扩展应用程序。

无论您是使用 JavaScript、Python 还是任何其他语言编写代码，Visual Studio Code 都可以让您轻松编写、调试和测试代码。

使用 JIRA，您可以创建和管理任务、跟踪进度并在截止日期之前保持领先。

使用 Slack，您可以发送消息、共享文件以及与他人实时协作。

使用 Webpack，您可以管理应用程序的资产、优化代码并轻松构建生产就绪的应用程序。

无论您是在处理小型项目还是大型代码库，Prettier 都能帮助您保持代码井井有条且易于阅读。

总之，这 10 个平台和工具是每个软件开发人员必备的。 无论您是刚刚起步还是拥有多年经验，这些工具都将帮助您更高效地完成工作。 无论您是在处理一个新项目、调试一个复杂的系统，还是只是想保持井井有条，这些工具都将帮助您完成工作。



原文:

As the world of software development continues to evolve and expand, it can be challenging for developers to keep up with the latest tools and technologies. However, being familiar with the most essential platforms and tools is crucial for success in the field. Whether you're a seasoned developer or just starting out, it's important to stay up-to-date on the latest advancements in software development. In this post, we'll explore the top 10 essential Platforms and Tools every software developer must be familiar with to stay ahead of the game.

1. Cloud Computing Platforms AWS, GCP, or Azure: AWS, GCP and Azure are cloud computing platforms that provide a wide range of services for hosting and scaling applications. Whether you're looking to host a new application, store data in the cloud, or manage a complex infrastructure, AWS, GCP and Azure have you covered.

2. Git: Git is a version control system that allows you to manage and track changes to your code. Whether you're working solo or as part of a team, Git helps you keep track of your codebase, collaborate with others, and revert changes when necessary.

3. GitHub: GitHub is a platform for hosting and collaborating on software projects. With GitHub, you can store your code in the cloud, collaborate with others, and track changes to your codebase.

4. Docker: Docker is a containerization platform that makes it easy to deploy and test applications. With Docker, you can create containers that are isolated from the underlying infrastructure, making it easier to manage and scale applications.

5. Visual Studio Code: Visual Studio Code is a versatile code editor that comes packed with a rich set of features and plugins. Whether you're writing code in JavaScript, Python, or any other language, Visual Studio Code makes it easy to write, debug, and test your code.

6. Postman: Postman is a must-have tool for any software developer working with APIs. Postman makes it easy to test and document APIs, and it also comes with a wide range of features for collaboration and documentation.

7. JIRA: JIRA is a project management tool that helps software development teams keep track of their work and stay organized. With JIRA, you can create and manage tasks, track progress, and stay on top of deadlines.

8. Slack: Slack is a communication tool that helps software development teams stay connected and organized. With Slack, you can send messages, share files, and collaborate with others in real-time.

9. Webpack: Webpack is a module bundler that makes it easy to manage dependencies and build applications. With Webpack, you can manage your application's assets, optimize your code, and build production-ready applications with ease.

10. Prettier: Prettier is a code formatter that helps you maintain a consistent code style. Whether you're working on a small project or a large codebase, Prettier helps you keep your code organized and easy to read.

In conclusion, these 10 platforms and tools must-haves for every software developer. Whether you're just starting out or have years of experience, these tools will help you get your work done more efficiently and effectively. Whether you're working on a new project, debugging a complex system, or just trying to stay organized, these tools will help you get the job done.
item.author: tarunmankar

item.title: 功能标志如何帮助您更快、更智能地发货？ | How Feature Flags Can Help You Ship Faster and Smarter?
item.description:
您是否厌倦了漫长的开发周期并且对将新功能投入生产犹豫不决？ 功能标志可能是您正在寻找的解决方案。 在这篇博文中，我们将探讨功能标志如何简化您的开发过程、降低错误风险，并让您更好地控制向用户发布的功能。 从测试新功能到向选定的用户组推出更改，功能标志可以帮助您完成这一切。 继续阅读以了解如何在今天的开发工作流程中开始使用功能标志。

让我们开始吧

什么是功能标志？

功能标志是一种控制用户可以看到哪些功能的方法。 您可以使用它们来试验新功能、收集数据并确保在向所有人推出它们之前一切正常。

将功能标志视为开关。 您可以使用功能标志来打开和关闭产品的功能，就像使用开关打开和关闭灯一样。 您不必为您的房子重新布线来切换灯，也不必更改代码来切换带有功能标志的功能。

使用功能标志，您可以在一小组用户上测试您的功能。 如果一切顺利并且您和那一小群人对新功能感到满意，您就可以向公众提供它。 这使您能够比以往更智能地开发产品😎。 此外，这种软件开发方式通常比传统软件开发更便宜。

什么是配置猫？

它允许软件工程师/开发人员远程控制其应用程序的行为，而无需重新部署代码。 这意味着可以测试新功能并逐步将其投入生产，并且无需使整个应用程序脱机即可修复错误。 借助 ConfigCat，开发人员可以使用功能标志来实施持续部署过程，使他们能够更快地发布新功能和更新，同时降低风险。

不仅如此，您还可以创建批量用户来定位特定用户。 例如，如果您希望为特定国家/地区的用户禁用某项功能或仅允许测试版用户访问某些功能，您可以创建组。

一些主要区别是：

免费增值 - ConfigCat慷慨的永久免费计划具有无限的团队规模、每月活跃用户和连接的实例，这是选择它们而不是 LaunchDarkly 的主要原因之一。
  • 团队规模——ConfigCat 为所有计划提供无限的团队规模，但 LaunchDarkly 按席位收费。
  • 此外，ConfigCat 向学生免费提供专业计划，并为教师提供定制定价计划。 老实说，ConfigCat 的定价计划比 LaunchDarkly 的更吸引我。

    • 隐私和安全

    • 而 LaunchDarkly 没有数据治理。

    在这里找出更多差异。

    在行动中看到它

    让我们学习如何在 React 项目中添加功能标志并使用 ConfigCat 管理它们。 为此，我构建了一个示例 React 项目，或者您可以尝试使用自己的项目。

    先决条件

    • 注册一个免费的ConfigCat 帐户。 该项目

      我们的示例应用程序是一个非常简单的笔记应用程序。

      • 从GitHub克隆/下载项目
      • 熟悉项目。
      • 使用此命令安装依赖项。
      • 打开浏览器并导航到localhost:3000 。

      在 ConfigCat 中创建功能标志

      打开 ConfigCat 仪表板开始创建功能标志。 已为您提供了一个名为isMyFirstFeatureEnabled的示例功能标志。 您可以通过单击X将其删除或保留它。
      单击+ 添加功能标志。

      为您的标志命名，使用isTitleFeatureEnabled作为键，将初始值设置为“On”，然后单击ADD FEATURE FLAG 。 将创建一个标志。

      配置 React 应用程序

      首先，安装configcat-react npm 包。

      启动您的编辑器并打开项目。 在项目的根目录中创建一个.env文件并包含此环境变量。 您可以通过单击右上角的VIEW SDK KEY按钮从 ConfigCat 仪表板获取 SDK 密钥。

       REACT_APP_CONFIGCAT_SDK_KEY=YOUR_CONFIGCAT_SDK_KEY

      打开位于/src文件夹中的index.js文件。 用ConfigCatProvider包装</App>组件

      <ConfigCatProvider  sdkKey={process.env.REACT_APP_CONFIGCAT_SDK_KEY}  pollingMode={PollingMode.AutoPoll}  options={{ pollIntervalSeconds: 30 }} >  <App /> </ConfigCatProvider>
      • 在这种情况下使用了PollingMode. Autopoll 。 在此模式下，ConfigCat SDK 会自动下载最新的值并将其保存在缓存中。 我们将pollIntervalSeconds设置为30 ，这意味着 ConfigCat 每 30 秒下载一次最新的值。 稍后将对此进行演示。 现在我们可以在我们的项目中使用功能标志。

      功能标志用法

      如果你尝试过这个项目，当你添加一个新的笔记时，你会给它一个标题。 因为笔记可能不需要标题，所以我们决定对测试版用户进行测试。 我们最终会根据测试版用户的反馈和使用情况扩展到所有用户。

      为此，让我们首先向标题输入添加一个功能标志。 此标题输入位于/src/components/CreateArea.jsx文件的第41行。

       ... import { useFeatureFlag } from "configcat-react"; ... function CreateArea(props) { ... const { value: isTitleFeatureEnabled, loading: isTitleFeatureEnabledLoading, } = useFeatureFlag("isTitleFeatureEnabled", false); ... return ( ... {!isTitleFeatureEnabledLoading && isTitleFeatureEnabled && ( <input onChange={handleChange} name="title" placeholder="Title" value={inputText.title} autoComplete="off" /> )} ... ) }
      • 我们上面所做的非常简单。 使用useFeatureFlag挂钩，我们首先获得isTitleFeatureEnabled标志值并在渲染 UI 时使用它们。 所以我们简单地隐藏和暴露 UI，这很好。

      您现在要做的就是在 ConfigCat 仪表板上切换标志。 更改将在 30 秒内反映出来。

      假设我们要为笔记添加删除功能。 但我们首先想在一个国家发布，然后逐步推广到其他国家。 让我们学习如何使用目标规则，但首先要添加一个功能标志。 我已经在 ConfigCat 仪表板中使用键isDeleteFeatureEnabled创建了一个标志。 所以继续创建这个标志。

      删除按钮及其实现位于/src/components/Note.jsx文件中。

       ... import { useFeatureFlag } from "configcat-react"; ... function CreateArea(props) { ... const userObject = {   identifier: "643564",   email: "rakesh@example.com",   country: "Japan", }; const { value: isDeleteFeatureEnabled, loading: isDeleteFeatureEnabledLoading, } = useFeatureFlag("isDeleteFeatureEnabled", false, userObject); function handleClick() { if (!isDeleteFeatureEnabled) { return; } props.deleteNote(props.id); } ... return ( ... {!isDeleteFeatureEnabledLoading && (     <button onClick={handleClick} disabled={!isDeleteFeatureEnabled}>      <DeleteIcon />     </button>   )} ... ) }
      • 在这里，我们不仅隐藏和暴露 UI，还禁用了删除功能。
      • 在上面的代码中，您看到了userObject 。 您需要传递此对象以使目标规则生效。 通常，我们从后端获取这些数据； 但是，因为这只是一个示例，所以我对值进行了硬编码。

      创建目标规则

      顾名思义，目标规则就是用来针对特定用户的规则。 让我们创建我们的第一个目标规则。
      点击添加新的定位规则。 从第一个下拉列表中选择Country ，从第二个下拉列表中选择IS ONE OF（明文） ，然后在下一个文本字段中填写您要发布此功能的国家/地区。

      要对此进行测试，请更改userObject中的国家/地区并查看。

      资源

      从这些资源中了解有关 ConfigCat 和功能标志的更多信息，

      • 配置猫博客
      • 结论

        功能标志（有时被粗略地和错误地称为功能切换开关）正在成为现代软件开发的重要组成部分。 无论您是试图使代码更加模块化的开发人员，还是希望更快地进行实验的产品经理，功能标志都可能对您有所帮助。 一般概念很简单：对于任何给定的功能，一个标志（打开或关闭）表示该功能是否应包含在应用程序中。 根据用例，该标志可以由开发过程中的几个不同参与者设置：根据业务价值审查新功能的产品所有者、想要执行不同测试场景的 QA 工程师或想要提供的最终用户他们对即将推出的版本的反馈。

        关注我了解更多。



    原文:

    Are you tired of long development cycles and hesitant to push new features to production? Feature flags may be the solution you're looking for. In this blog post, we'll explore how feature flags can streamline your development process, reduce the risk of errors, and give you more control over the features you release to your users. From testing new features to rolling out changes to a select group of users, feature flags can help you do it all. Keep reading to learn how you can start using feature flags in your development workflow today.

    Let's Get Started

    What are Feature Flags?

    Feature flags are a way to control what features your users see. You can use them to experiment with new features, collect data and make sure things are working correctly before you roll them out to everyone.

    Consider feature flags to be switches. You can use feature flags to turn on and off features of your product in the same way that you would turn on and off lights using a switch. You don't have to rewire your house to toggle the light, and you don't have to make code changes to toggle features with feature flags.

    With feature flags, you may test your features on a small group of users. If all goes well and you and that small group of people are satisfied with the new feature, you can make it available to the general public. This enables you to develop your product smarter than ever before 😎. Also, this style of software development is usually less expensive than traditional software development.

    What is ConfigCat?

    ConfigCat is a feature flag management service and LaunchDarkly alternative. It allows software engineers/developers to remotely control the behavior of their applications without needing to redeploy the code. This means that new features can be tested and rolled out to production gradually, and bugs can be fixed without having to take the entire application offline. With ConfigCat, developers can use feature flags to implement a continuous deployment process, enabling them to release new features and updates faster, with less risk.

    Not only that, but you can also create batches of users to target specific users. You can create groups if, for example, you wish to disable a feature for users in a specific country or only enable beta users to access certain features.

    ConfigCat Vs LaunchDarkly

    Both ConfigCat and LaunchDarkly are feature flag management services that allow developers to remotely control the behavior of their applications without needing to redeploy the code. Some of the key differences are:

    LaunchDarkly pricing Vs ConfigCat

    • Freemium - ConfigCat's generous forever free plan with unlimited team size, monthly active users, and connected instances is one of the main reasons to choose them over LaunchDarkly.
    • Team Size - ConfigCat provides unlimited team size for all plans, but LaunchDarkly charges per seat.
    • ConfigCat costs by the number of feature flags, whereas LaunchDarkly charges by the number of monthly active users (only client side).
    • Furthermore, ConfigCat offers its pro plan for free to students and has a custom pricing plan for teachers. ConfigCat's pricing plan is honestly more appealing to me than LaunchDarkly's.

    Privacy and Security

    • ConfigCat includes security features (2FA, SSO, SAML, and Active Directory) in all of their plans, whereas LaunchDarkly requires a fee and most security features are only available in the pro and enterprise subscriptions. And LaunchDarkly has no data governance.

    Find out more differences here.

    See it in Action

    Let's learn how to add feature flags in a React project and manage them with ConfigCat. For this, I built a sample React project or you can try with your own project.

    Prerequisites

    • Sign up for a free ConfigCat account. (If you are a student you can opt for pro plan)
    • Basics of React are good to have but not necessary.

    The Project

    Our sample application is a very simple note-taking app.

    • Clone/download the project from the GitHub
    • Familiarize yourself with the project.
    • Install the dependencies with this command.
    • Open a browser and navigate to localhost:3000.

    Creating feature flags in ConfigCat

    Open the ConfigCat dashboard to begin creating feature flags. An example feature flag called isMyFirstFeatureEnabled has been provided for you. You can either delete it by clicking the X or leave it alone.
    Click + ADD FEATURE FLAG.

    Give your flag a name, use isTitleFeatureEnabled for the key, set the initial values to "On," and then click ADD FEATURE FLAG. A flag will be created.

    Configure React app

    First, install the configcat-react npm package.

    Launch your editor and open the project. Create a .env file in the project's root directory and include this environment variable. You can obtain the SDK key from the ConfigCat dashboard by clicking the VIEW SDK KEY button in the upper-right corner.

    REACT_APP_CONFIGCAT_SDK_KEY=YOUR_CONFIGCAT_SDK_KEY

    Open the index.js file which is located in the /src folder. Wrap the </App> component with ConfigCatProvider

    <ConfigCatProvider
    sdkKey={process.env.REACT_APP_CONFIGCAT_SDK_KEY}
    pollingMode={PollingMode.AutoPoll}
    options={{ pollIntervalSeconds: 30 }}
  >
    <App />
</ConfigCatProvider>
    • pollingMode - Polling is a method for retrieving setting values from ConfigCat. PollingMode. Autopoll was used in this case. In this mode, ConfigCat SDK automatically downloads the most recent values and keeps them in the cache. We set pollIntervalSeconds to 30, which means ConfigCat downloads the most recent values every 30 seconds. This will be demonstrated in a moment. Now we can use feature flags in our project.

    Feature flags usage

    1. Title feature

    If you tried the project, when you add a new note, you give it a title. Because a note may not require a title, we decided to test it with beta users. We will eventually extend out to all users based on feedback and usage from beta users.

    To do so, let's first add a feature flag to the title input. This title input is located in /src/components/CreateArea.jsx file and at line 41.

    ...
import { useFeatureFlag } from "configcat-react";
... function CreateArea(props) { ... const { value: isTitleFeatureEnabled, loading: isTitleFeatureEnabledLoading, } = useFeatureFlag("isTitleFeatureEnabled", false); ... return ( ... {!isTitleFeatureEnabledLoading && isTitleFeatureEnabled && ( <input onChange={handleChange} name="title" placeholder="Title" value={inputText.title} autoComplete="off" /> )} ... )
}
    • What we did above is really straightforward. Using the useFeatureFlag hook, we first obtained the isTitleFeatureEnabled flag values and used them while rendering the UI. So we simply hide and expose the UI, which is good.

    All you have to do now is toggle flags on the ConfigCat dashboard. Changes will be reflected within 30 seconds.

    2. Delete feature

    Let's say we are adding a delete feature to notes. But we first want to release it in one country and gradually roll it out to other countries. Let's learn how to use target rules but first add a feature flag. I already created a flag in the ConfigCat dashboard with the key isDeleteFeatureEnabled. So go ahead and create this flag.

    The delete button and its implementation are located in /src/components/Note.jsx file.

    ...
import { useFeatureFlag } from "configcat-react";
... function CreateArea(props) { ... const userObject = {     identifier: "643564",     email: "rakesh@example.com",     country: "Japan",
  };
  const { value: isDeleteFeatureEnabled, loading: isDeleteFeatureEnabledLoading, } = useFeatureFlag("isDeleteFeatureEnabled", false, userObject); function handleClick() { if (!isDeleteFeatureEnabled) { return; } props.deleteNote(props.id); } ... return ( ... {!isDeleteFeatureEnabledLoading && (         <button onClick={handleClick} disabled={!isDeleteFeatureEnabled}>           <DeleteIcon />         </button>
      )} ... )
}
    • Here, we not only hide and expose UI but also disable the delete functionality.
    • In the above code, you see userObject. You need to pass this object to make the target rules to work. Usually, we get this data from the backend; however, because this is only an example, I hard-coded values.

    Creating target rules

    As the name suggests, target rules are the rules used to target specific users. Let's create our first target rule.
    Click ADD NEW TARGETING RULE. Select Country from the first dropdown, IS ONE OF (cleartext) from the second dropdown, and fill in the next text field with the countries where you want to release this feature.

    To test this out, change the country in userObject and see.

    Resources

    Learn more about ConfigCat and feature flags from these resources,

    • ConfigCat blog
    • ConfigCat SDK references

    Conclusion

    Feature flags (sometimes loosely and erroneously referred to as feature toggle switches) are becoming a crucial component of modern software development. Whether you're a developer trying to make your code more modular, or a product manager looking to experiment more quickly, it's likely that feature flags can help you. The general concept is simple: for any given feature, a flag—either on or off—signals whether that functionality should be included in the application or not. Depending on the use case, that flag can be set by several different actors in the development process: the product owner who's vetting new features based on business value, a QA engineer who wants to exercise different test scenarios or an end user who wants to give their feedback on an upcoming version.

    Follow me for more.
item.author: itsrakesh

item.title: Ruby on Rails 终极指南：了解其优势、未来和职业机会 | The Ultimate Guide to Ruby on Rails: Understanding its Benefits, Future, and Career Opportunities
item.description:
您在寻找 Ruby on Rails 的综合指南吗？ 如果是这样，那么您来对地方了。 在这篇博文中，我们将介绍 Ruby on Rails 的基础知识和优势、其潜在的未来发展以及 Ruby on Rails 培训可以提供哪些职业机会。 我们还将讨论学习 Ruby on Rails 最重要的注意事项以及入门的最佳资源。 到本文结束时，您将更好地了解学习 Ruby on Rails 所带来的所有可能性。

什么是 Ruby on Rails (ROR)？

它提供了一种使用模型-视图-控制器 (MVC) 架构模式构建 Web 应用程序的结构。

强大的第三方库：它拥有庞大的开发人员社区，他们通过开发和共享 gems（库）为框架做出贡献。

  • 出色的文档：Rails 拥有全面且编写良好的文档，使新开发人员更容易上手。

    • 了解 Ruby on Rails 的优势

    它为开发人员提供了一系列特性和功能，使其成为一种有吸引力的技术，可以包含在他们的项目中。 使用Ruby on Rails 培训可以为开发人员提供许多优势。 它是一个高效的框架，允许开发人员快速轻松地创建复杂的应用程序。 此外，Ruby on Rails 框架具有高度可扩展性，允许开发人员定制他们的应用程序以满足他们的需要。

    例如，Rails 包含一个强大的 ORM（对象关系映射器），它允许开发人员快速、轻松地访问数据库，从而减少开发所需的时间。 所有这些优势使 Ruby on Rails 成为对开发人员具有吸引力的技术

    它自 2000 年代中期以来一直存在，并且一直是现代网络发展的关键部分。
    随着网络的不断发展，Ruby on Rails 也在不断发展。 对 Web 应用程序的需求不断增长，以及 Web 的复杂性不断增加，推动了这项技术的发展。 在不久的将来，Ruby on Rails 开发人员可以期待看到新工具的兴起，这些工具可以使开发更快、更高效。 他们还将能够受益于一系列新功能，例如更好的测试和调试功能、更高的安全性以及更容易与其他网络技术集成。

    随着该框架越来越受欢迎，该领域的职业机会也越来越多。

    对于希望专注于 Ruby on Rails 的开发人员，有多种职业道路可供选择。 许多公司正在寻找在使用该框架构建自定义 Web 应用程序方面具有专业知识的开发人员，以及具有开发和优化基于 Web 的数据库的经验的开发人员。 此外，可以聘请有经验的开发人员作为顾问，为使用该框架的其他开发人员提供指导和支持。

    对于那些希望教导他人的人，也有机会。 现在有各种在线课程可供那些希望学习 Ruby on Rails 的人使用。 随着该框架越来越受欢迎，越来越多的课程被提供，让开发人员有机会与那些希望学习的人分享他们的专业知识。

    最后，对于那些希望走在行业前沿的人来说，还有很多机会。 对于那些希望突破框架界限的人，有机会从事机器学习和人工智能等项目

    结论

    它是一个强大而灵活的框架，允许快速开发并拥有一个庞大的支持社区。 此外，它易于学习，语法清晰易读。 凭借其受欢迎程度和多功能性，学习 Ruby on Rails 可以在科技行业开辟许多工作机会。



    原文:

    Are you looking for a comprehensive guide to Ruby on Rails? If so, then you’ve come to the right place. In this blog post, we’ll cover the basics and benefits of Ruby on Rails, its potential future developments, and what kinds of career opportunities Ruby on Rails training can provide. We’ll also discuss the most important considerations for learning Ruby on Rails and the best resources for getting started. By the end of this post, you’ll have a better understanding of all the possibilities that come with learning Ruby on Rails.

    What is Ruby on Rails (ROR)?

    Ruby on Rails (ROR or Rails) is an open-source web framework written in the Ruby programming language. It provides a structure for building web applications using the Model-View-Controller (MVC) architectural pattern.

    Key features and benefits of Rails include:

    • Convention over Configuration (CoC): It streamlines the development process by providing defaults for common tasks and reducing the amount of code required.
    • MVC architecture: It separates the application into three components - models, views, and controllers - making it easier to develop, maintain, and test.
    • Active Record pattern: It provides an ORM (Object Relational Mapping) layer that simplifies database management.
    • Robust Third-party libraries: It has a large community of developers that contribute to the framework by developing and sharing gems (libraries).
    • Excellent documentation: Rails has comprehensive and well-written documentation making it easier for new developers to get started.

    Understanding the Advantages of Ruby on Rails

    The Ruby programming language serves as the foundation for Ruby on Rails, an open-source web application framework. It offers developers a range of features and capabilities that make it an attractive technology to include in their projects. Ruby on Rails is known for its scalability, flexibility, and speed of development, making it a popular choice for web applications.

    Using Ruby on Rails training can provide many advantages to developers. It is an efficient framework that allows developers to create complex applications quickly and easily. The syntax of Ruby is designed to be highly readable and concise, allowing developers to create powerful applications with minimal code. Additionally, the Ruby on Rails framework is highly extensible, allowing developers to customize their applications to fit their needs.

    Ruby on Rails also provides a wide range of features and libraries that make it easier for developers to implement their projects. For example, Rails includes a powerful ORM (Object Relational Mapper) that allows developers too quickly and easily access databases, thus reducing the time needed for development. Rails also include a wide range of libraries and tools that allow developers to quickly and easily implement different features such as authentication and authorization.
    All these advantages make Ruby on Rails an attractive technology for developers

    The Future of Ruby on Rails

    Ruby on Rails is one of the most popular web application frameworks currently available. It has been around since the mid-2000s and has been a key part of the development of the modern web.
    As the web continues to evolve, so too does Ruby on Rails. This technology is being driven forward by the growing demand for web applications, as well as by the increasing complexity of the web. The future of Ruby on Rails is likely to feature innovations in web development, such as better user experience design, improved scalability, and enhanced development speed.

    In the near future, Ruby on Rails developers can expect to see a rise in new tools that make development faster and more efficient. They will also be able to benefit from a range of new features, such as better testing and debugging capabilities, improved security, and easier integration with other web technologies.

    Ruby on Rails Career Opportunities

    Ruby on Rails is an increasingly popular web development framework that has been used to power a variety of web applications. As the framework continues to gain in popularity, so too do the career opportunities in the field.

    For developers looking to specialize in Ruby on Rails, there are a variety of career paths available. Many companies are looking for developers with expertise in building custom web applications using the framework, as well as those with experience developing and optimizing web-based databases. In addition, experienced developers may be hired as consultants to provide guidance and support to other developers using the framework.

    For those looking to teach others, there are also opportunities available. There are now a variety of online courses available for those looking to learn Ruby on Rails. As the framework continues to grow in popularity, more courses are being offered, giving developers the chance to share their expertise with those looking to learn.

    Finally, for those looking to be at the cutting edge of the industry, there are plenty of opportunities. Web startups are increasingly turning to Ruby on Rails for their development needs, giving developers the chance to work on innovative projects. For those looking to push the boundaries of the framework, there are opportunities to work on projects such as machine learning and artificial intelligence

    Conclusion

    Ruby on Rails is a great choice for your career because it is a popular and in-demand technology, widely used for web development. It is a powerful and flexible framework that allows for rapid development and has a large, supportive community. Additionally, it is easy to learn and has a clean, readable syntax. With its popularity and versatility, learning Ruby on Rails can open up many job opportunities in the tech industry.
item.author: prishav

item.title: 醒来 | Wake up
item.description:
今天，我在化学实验室测验中得了低分，而且我很确定我的化学期中考试成绩也不好。 老实说，这些课程感觉很简单，这让我在考试中挣扎时感觉更糟。

这是一个相当艰难的开始，考虑到我回到大学的头几周（在 1 个空档年之后）要打开很多行李并将很多东西搬到一个远离我在世界另一端的家的全新地方。

现在我想我把这些东西写下来可能是在浪费宝贵的时间。 我不确定这是出于冲动还是出于动机。 我所知道的是我需要退后一步，呼吸，然后写作。

计时器归零，它击中了我。 我在做什么？ 这次化学考试对我来说应该很容易。 它不是。 我冻僵了，对未来变得紧张和焦虑。
这些想法出现，它变得沉重。 我感到瘫痪了。

我应该怎么办？ 我问我自己。 我应该怎么办？！ 现在在我的床罩上尖叫的同时不顾一切地问自己。

住口。

呼吸。

接受。

理解。

行为。

抱歉造成混乱，我只是需要在脑子里腾出一些空间。 说真的，我想成长。 我想承诺。 我写这个有点非正式的博客或“尖叫”是因为我想把事情说出来。 换句话说，我希望能够接受那部分的我，所以我把它写下来，看了一会儿。 看起来很令人沮丧，然后它实际上变得有点有趣。 我本可以做得更好。

那么为什么我没有做得更好呢？ 好吧，这是清单：

  • 手机平均使用屏幕时间 4 到 5 小时
  • 超级晚睡
  • 起床超级晚
  • 学习时容易分心
  • 担心很多其他事情而不是手头的任务
  • 不运动
  • 视频游戏（但我刚在 Steam 上找到了蜘蛛侠）
  • 最后一分钟做硬件

你有它。 过度刺激的恶棍。 我们也称他们为零注意力小队。 但是，是的，这真的花了我很多时间。

现在，尽管听起来很俗气，但我会反击。 我想成为一个更好的人。 我想学习因为我想学习，所以我现在告诉自己不要再死盯着成绩，把注意力转移到真正需要掌握的东西上。

我会努力为自己灌输成长心态。 我保证在发表这篇文章后，我会把愚蠢的自我扔到窗外，驱使自己努力工作和学习，而不仅仅是出于动力。

将来，我只想帮助其他人（特别是在技术/编程方面）。 如果我不愿意为此做出牺牲，我就不能那样做。

我会。

祝福。

 console.log("hello world");


原文:

Today, I got a low grade on a chem lab quiz and I'm quite sure I didn't do well on the chem midterm too. The lessons felt pretty easy honestly which made it even feel worse when I struggled in the exam.

It's a pretty rough start considering my first few weeks back in college (after 1 gap year) was a lot of unpacking and moving a lot of stuff to a whole new place far away from my home in the other side of the world.

Right now I think I'm probably wasting valuable time by writing these things down. I'm not sure if its out of impulse or motivation. All I know is that I need to take a step back, breathe, and write.

The timer went to zero and it hit me. What am I doing? This chem exam was supposed to be easy for me. It was not. I was freezing up, getting nervous and anxious about the future.
"I still need to write my essay for my university applications (currently in community college). I need to relearn my calculus hard since prof is making it so difficult. I need to get good grades for university and I'm already having this BAD START. But GRAHH, I'm always on my phone, wasting time in that world. Moreover, I want to get back on my coding. I don't want to lose that skill. How can I manage school and coding? I want to learn this and that algorithm. I want to try developing on the blockchain. What about my physical health? I need to do something about that too. I need to make SOMETHING happen." These thoughts come up and it gets heavy. I feel paralyzed.

What should I do? I ask myself. WHAT SHOULD I DO?! Now importunately asking myself while screaming on my bed covers.

Shut up.

Breathe.

Accept.

Understand.

Act.

Sorry about that chaos, I just needed to make some space in my head. Genuinely, I want to grow. I want to commit. I wrote this somewhat informal blog or "scream" because I wanted to let things out. In other words, I wanted to be able to accept that part of me so I placed it into words and looked at it for a while. It was frustrating to look at and then it actually got kind of funny. I could've done better.

"Could've done better."

So why didn't I do better? Well, here's the list:

  • 4hrs to 5hrs average screen time on phone
  • sleep super late
  • wake up super late
  • easily distracted while studying
  • worrying a lot about other things instead of the task at hand already
  • not exercising
  • video games (but mehn i just got spiderman on steam)
  • doing hw last minute

There you have it. The overstimulation villains. Let's also call them the zero attention span squad. But yep, it really took a lot of me.

Now, as cheesy as it may sound, I will fight back. I want to become a better person. I want to learn because I want to learn, so I'm telling myself now to stop fixating on the grade and shift the focus on what actually needs to be grasped.

I will work hard on instilling a growth mindset for myself. I promise after publishing this post, I will throw my stupid ego out the window and drive myself to work hard and learn instead of just doing it out of motivation.

In the future, I just want to help other people (specifically with tech/programming). And I can't do that if I'm not willing to make the sacrifices for it.

I will.

Blessed.

console.log("hello world");

item.title: 从 Webseite zu Excel 中删除数据 | Scrapen Daten aus Webseite zu Excel
item.description:
文章来源：从 Webseite zu Excel Sie kennen wahrscheinlich，wie die grundlegenden Funktionen von Excel zu verwenden sind，ob Sie eine digitale Einheimische oder einer digitalen Einwanderer sind。 所以 fragen Sie vielleicht danach，wie wir denn automatisch Daten extrahieren und sie aus Weseite zu Excel scrapen können？

方法 1：Erhalten Webdaten mit Excel Web 查询

方法 2：Erhalten Webdaten mit Excel VBA

点击 HIER，um mit VBA eine Aufgabe in excel 2010 zu beginnen。

方法 3：Automatisierte Web-Scraping-Tools

方法 4：Auslagern Sie Ihr Web-Scraping-Projekt

奖金： $0~$249 亲 Monat

链接： dataservice.octoparse.com/de/web-scrapi ...

永恒的开发帖子：

我多年前就希望知道的 Git 概念

原文:

Artikel Quelle: Scrapen Daten aus Webseite zu Excel Sie kennen wahrscheinlich, wie die grundlegenden Funktionen von Excel zu verwenden sind, ob Sie eine digitale Einheimische oder einer digitalen Einwanderer sind. Es ist einfach, mit Excel Daten zu sortieren, filtern, gliedern, und Diagrammen zu erstellen. Voraussichtlich können Sie noch erweiterte Datenanalysen mit Pivot- und Regressionsmodellen bekommen.

Das ist eine mühelose Aufgabe, solange die Daten in ein strukturiertes Format umgewandelt werden. Das Problem ist, wie man skalierbare Daten extrahieren und sie in Excel einfügen kann. Dies könnte eine mühsame Aufgabe werden, wenn man es manuell durch wiederholtes Tippen, Suchen, Kopieren und Einfügen macht. So fragen Sie vielleicht danach, wie wir denn automatisch Daten extrahieren und sie aus Weseite zu Excel scrapen können?

In diesem Artikel würde ich einige Möglichkeiten vorstellen, damit Sie Webdaten aus Webseite zu Excel scrapen und Ihre Zeit sparen können.

Methode 1: Erhalten Webdaten mit Excel Web Queries

Im Vergleich zu der durch Kopieren und Einfügen manuell umwandelnden Transformation von Webdaten kann man mit Excel Web Queries(Englisch) schnell Daten einer Webseite in ein Excel-Arbeitsblatt umwandeln. Es kann automatisch Tabellen erkennen, die in den HTML-Code der Webseite eingebettet sind. Excel Web Queries können auch verwendet werden, wenn eine Standard-ODBC (Open Database Connectivity) Verbindung schwer zu erstellen oder zu pflegen ist. Mit Excel Web Queries können Sie direkt Tabellen von beliebigen Webseiten scrapen.

Den Prozess kann man in einigen einfachen Schritten zusammenfassen (Für ausführliche Informationen lesen Sie bitte diesen Artikel(Englisch)): ✅ Schritt 1: Gehen zu Daten > Externe Daten erhalten> Aus dem Web ✅ Schritt 2: Ein Browser-Fenster mit dem Namen "Neue Web Query" wird angezeigt

✅ Schritt 3: Geben Sie in die Adressleiste die Webadresse ein.

✅ Schritt 4: Die Webseite wird geladen und zeigt gelbe Symbole von Daten/Tabellen an. ✅ Schritt 5: Wählen Sie das entsprechende Symbol aus.

✅ Schritt 6: Drücken Sie den Button „Importieren“.

Jetzt haben Sie die Webdaten zum Excel-Arbeitsblatt gescrapt, die perfekt in Zeilen und Spalten wie gewünscht angeordnet werden.

Methode 2: Erhalten Webdaten mit Excel VBA

Man verwendet Formeln in Excel (z.B. =avg(...), =sum(...), =if(...), usw.) oft , aber die meisten sind weniger vertraut mit der eingebauten Sprache - Visual Basic for Application (VBA). Es ist allgemein als „Makros“ bekannt und solche Excel-Dateien werden als **.xlsm gespeichert. Bevor Sie die Sprache verwenden, müssen Sie zuerst den Tab „Developer“ in der Multifunktionsleiste aktivieren (Rechtsklick auf Datei -> Multifunktionsleiste anpassen -> Tab „Developer“ aktivieren). Richten Sie dann Ihr Layout ein. In diesem Entwickler-Interface können Sie VBA-Code schreiben, der verschiedene Ereignisse befestigen kann. Klicken Sie HIER, um mit VBA eine Aufgabe in excel 2010 zu beginnen.

Die Verwendung von Excel VBA wird ein bisschen komplizierter - das ist nicht so benutzerfreundlich für die Nicht-Programmierer. VBA funktioniert durch die Ausführung von Makros und Schritt-für-Schritt-Prozeduren, die in Excel Visual Basic geschrieben sind. Um mit VBA Daten von Webseiten zu scrapen, müssen wir manche VBA-Skripts vorbereiten, um Anfragen an Webseiten zu senden und die zurückgegebenen Daten von diesen Webseiten zu erhalten. Es ist üblich, VBA mit XMLHTTP und regulären Ausdrücken zu verwenden, um die Webseiten zu parsen. Mit Windows-Betriebssystem können Sie VBA mit WinHTTP oder InternetExplorer zusammmen verwenden, um Daten von Webseiten zu Excel zu scrapen.

Mit Geduld und Übungen können Sie Excel-VBA-Code und HTML-Kenntnisse beherrschen. Und es lohnt sich, die Sprache zu erlernen, weil sie das Web-Scraping zu Excel viel einfacher und effizienter machen kann und die wiederholende Arbeit automatisieren lässt. Es gibt viele Materialien und Foren, in denen Sie lernen können, wie man VBA-Code schreiben sollte.

Methode 3: Automatisierte Web-Scraping-Tools

Für jemanden, der ein einfaches Tool sucht, um Daten von Webseiten zu scrapen, aber den VBA-Code nicht selbst einrichten möchte, empfehle ich dringend automatisierte Web Scraping Tools wie Octoparse, um Daten direkt in Ihr Excel-Arbeitsblatt oder über API zu exportieren. Es ist keine Programmierkenntnisse erforderlich. Natürlich können Sie auch eine Web-Scraping-Freeware aus Top 20 Web Crawling Tools auswählen und sofort mit der Datenextraktion und dem Daten-Exportieren beginnen. Verschiedene Web-Scraping-Tools haben ihre Vor- und Nachteile, sodass Sie ein Tool nach Ihren Bedürfnissen auswählen könnten. Das folgende Video zeigt, wie Sie ein automatisiertes Web-Scraping-Tool nutzen können, um Webdaten effizient zu extrahieren.

Methode 4: Auslagern Sie Ihr Web-Scraping-Projekt

Wenn Zeit für Sie am wichtigsten ist und Sie sich auf Ihr Kerngeschäft konzentrieren möchten, wäre es die beste Wahl, dem professionellen und erfahrenden Web-Scraping-Team solch komplizierte Web-Scraping-Arbeiten auszulagern. Es ist schwierig, Daten von Webseiten zu scrapen, weil Anti-Scraping-Bots von manchen Webseiten das Web-Scraping einschränkt. Ein kompetentes Web-Scraping-Team können Ihnen dabei helfen, Daten von Webseiten auf korrekte Art und Weise zu extrahieren und Ihnen strukturierte Daten in einer Excel-Tabelle oder in einem beliebigen Format zu senden.

Hier bekommen Sie Octoparse! 🤩

Preis: $0~$249 pro Monat

Packet & Preise: Octoparse Premium-Preise & Verpackung

Kostenlose Testversion: 14-tägige kostenlose Testversion

Herunterladen: Octoparse für Windows und MacOs

Link: dataservice.octoparse.com/de/web-scrapi…

Git Concepts I Wish I Knew Years Ago

Michael Walmsley - Jan 29

💡Piyush Kesarwani - Jan 30

item.title: 什么是亚马逊 S3？ | What is Amazon S3?
item.description:
希曼舒·古普塔

发表于 1 月 31 日

亚马逊简单存储服务 (S3) 是一种云存储服务。 这是对象存储，我们可以保存任何类型的数据，这些数据将以对象的形式存储，并且可以从互联网上的任何地方检索。 我们可以使用 AWS 控制台处理我们所有的项目，它提供 99.99% 的可用性和 99.999% 的持久性。 你可以在这里阅读更多。

首先让我们在 AWS 控制台的帮助下制作我们的 S3 存储桶，登录到您的控制台，然后在顶部的搜索栏中搜索 S3 并单击它。

为您的存储桶起一个唯一的名称，选择您的服务器并取消选中阻止所有公共访问设置，然后单击创建您的存储桶将被创建。

为您的存储桶起一个唯一的名称，选择您的服务器并取消选中阻止所有公共访问设置，然后单击创建您的存储桶将被创建。

原文:

Himanshu Gupta

Posted on Jan 31

Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3) is a cloud storage service. It is object storage here we can save any type of data that will be stored in the form of objects and can be retrieved from anywhere over the internet. We can handle all our items using the AWS console, it provides 99.99 % availability and 99.999% durability. You can read more here.

First let’s make our S3 bucket with the help of AWS console, sign-in to your console and then search for S3 in the search bar on the top and click on it.

![Image description](dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads…


Give your bucket a unique name, select your server and uncheck the block all public access settings and then click on create your bucket will be created.

Give your bucket a unique name, select your server and uncheck the block all public access settings and then click on create your bucket will be created.

Now after making S3 bucket and generating the credentials, Let’s get started with writing code for uploading of image.

Flder Permission

You are getting this error because the file is set to private and you don’t have the permission do download it. To do so, you must set a Bucket Policy according to the permissions you with to use.

If you want any user to be able to download bucket objects, you can set a policy such as:

{ "Version":"2012-10-17", "Statement":[ { "Sid":"PublicRead", "Effect":"Allow",

"Principal": "", "Action":["s3:GetObject"],

"Resource":["arn:aws:s3:::examplebucket/

"]

} ] }

Another option, if you want to serve content, is to use Amazon CloudFront as a CDN.

Now after making S3 bucket and generating the credentials, Let’s get started with writing code for uploading of image.

Flder Permission

You are getting this error because the file is set to private and you don’t have the permission do download it. To do so, you must set a Bucket Policy according to the permissions you with to use.

If you want any user to be able to download bucket objects, you can set a policy such as:

{ "Version":"2012-10-17", "Statement":[ { "Sid":"PublicRead", "Effect":"Allow",

"Principal": "", "Action":["s3:GetObject"],

"Resource":["arn:aws:s3:::examplebucket/

"]

} ] }

Another option, if you want to serve content, is to use Amazon CloudFront as a CDN.

Let’s install the dependecies. We are going to use the official AWS software development kit.

npm i — save aws-sdk
Here is our code to upload files in S3.

const AWS = require(‘aws-sdk’); const fs = require(‘fs’); const AWSCredentials = { accessKey: ‘YOUR_ACCESS_KEY’, secret: ‘YOUR_SECRET_KEY’, bucketName: ‘YOUR_S3_BUCKET_NAME’ }; const s3 = new AWS.S3({ accessKeyId: AWSCredentials.accessKey, secretAccessKey: AWSCredentials.secret }); const uploadToS3 = (fileName) => { // Read content from the file const fileContent = fs.readFileSync(fileName); // Setting up S3 upload parameters const params = { Bucket: AWSCredentials.bucketName, Key: fileName, Body: fileContent }; // Uploading files to the bucket s3.upload(params, function(err, data) { if (err) { throw err; }

console.log(File uploaded successfully. ${data.Location});

}); };

uploadToS3(“file.jpg”); // file to upload

Let’s see how this code works.

First, we are loading our node module and creating a configuration object.

const AWS = require(‘aws-sdk’); const fs = require(‘fs’); const AWSCredentials = { accessKey: ‘YOUR_ACCESS_KEY’, secret: ‘YOUR_SECRET_KEY’, bucketName: ‘YOUR_S3_BUCKET_NAME’ };

Copy your access key and secret from the credentials files and paste it here. Put the bucket name in the configuration file. Then, we are creating a S3 object by providing it the access key and secret.

const s3 = new AWS.S3({ accessKeyId: AWSCredentials.accessKey, secretAccessKey: AWSCredentials.secret });

Then, we are creating a function to upload the file in S3 bucket. The function takes file name as an input and expect the file to present in the same foler where our code is.

First, we are reading the file as a Buffer using the following
code.

const fileContent = fs.readFileSync(fileName);

Then, we are creating an object with buffer, file name and bucket name and passing it in the upload() function.

Let’s run the code and check it’s output.

node app.js

This should return the following response.

File uploaded successfully. cfgdemo.s3.amazonaws.com/file.jpg

You can view the file in the S3 bucket as well.

Then, we are creating an object with buffer, file name and bucket name and passing it in the upload() function.

Let’s run the code and check it’s output.

node app.js

This should return the following response.

File uploaded successfully. cfgdemo.s3.amazonaws.com/file.jpg

You can view the file in the S3 bucket as well.


)
